title: xyris consanguinea
intro: Xyris consanguinea, also known as the yellow-eyed grass, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Xyridaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The yellow-eyed grass has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is also used as an ingredient in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
